title: 4. Main Window Tour
description: A map of the modern Neural Companion UI: runtime controls, chat, persona, chunking, dry run, tutorials, and addon-backed panels.

steps.title: One Modern GUI
steps.body: Neural Companion now starts into the modern main.ui interface. The old legacy GUI is no longer the user-facing path, so these tutorials focus on this single layout.

steps.title: Host Controls
steps.body: The host controls choose the avatar engine, model, input mode, stream mode, TTS backend, and other runtime essentials. This is where most sessions begin.

steps.title: Operational Buttons
steps.body: INITIALIZE SYSTEM starts the companion runtime. Terminate Session stops it. Use these buttons instead of closing the app when you want to change core runtime settings.

steps.title: Chat Area
steps.body: The chat panel is the ordinary conversation surface. Use it to test the model and speech path before adding visual replies, avatars, or background sensory behavior.

steps.title: Persona Tab
steps.body: The Persona tab controls presets, system prompt, voice selection, emotional instructions, and TTS behavior. Presets are the fastest way to switch character setups.

steps.title: Chunking Tab
steps.body: Chunking controls how text is split for speech and avatar timing. The defaults are intended to work; use Dry Run before manual tuning when possible.

steps.title: Dry Run Tab
steps.body: Dry Run measures the active configuration and can recommend better startup and streaming values for your machine.

steps.title: Tutorials Tab
steps.body: Tutorials are loaded from JSON files, so the manual can grow without hardcoding every walkthrough into the app.
